questions.optionB: Recurrent, unexpected panic attacks (sudden surge of intense fear/discomfort reaching peak within minutes) with persistent worry about future attacks or behavioral change
questions.explanation: Panic disorder: recurrent, UNEXPECTED panic attacks + ≥1 month of persistent worry about future attacks/consequences OR maladaptive behavioral change. Panic attack (≥4 of 13 symptoms): palpitations, sweating, trembling, SOB, choking sensation, chest pain, nausea, dizziness, derealization/depersonalization, fear of losing control/dying, numbness/tingling, chills/hot flushes. Peak: within minutes. Must rule out medical causes (hyperthyroidism, pheochromocytoma, cardiac arrhythmias). Treatment: SSRIs (first-line long-term), benzodiazepines (acute relief — alprazolam, clonazepam), CBT. Reference: Niraj Ahuja — Panic Disorder.
